title: Figure critique + grade (one critic, one iteration)
description: What the fresh critic returns each iteration: an independent 1-5 grade per rubric axis, an aggregated 0-100 score, the pass decision with hard gates, plus executable findings the generator addresses next. The critic re-derives each axis from the rendered figure + data + frozen <goals> and spot-checks numbers itself; it does not trust that a 'fixed' item was fixed. One file per iteration at <sandbox_root>/iter<N>/critique.json.

axes.description: One entry per active axis. Omit 'domain' entirely when the figure makes no external domain claim (n_axes drops to 4).

overall_score.description: 100 × (Σ axis scores) / (5 × n_axes). n_axes = 5, or 4 when 'domain' is absent. Equal weight, no per-axis weighting.

gate_failures.description: Confirmed hard-gate violations that force pass=false regardless of overall_score: a figure value that contradicts the data, a misleading axis (truncated/dual/hidden-log), or fabricated data presented as real. The critic must independently confirm each before listing it.

spotchecks.description: Independent verifications the critic performed this round (NOT trusting that a 'fixed' item was fixed).

target.description: What was checked, e.g. 'group-B bar height = 2.4' or 'MAPK panel includes ERK/MEK'.

method.description: How, e.g. 'recomputed from data/levels.csv', 'lit snippet: KEGG MAPK members'.

findings.description: Executable fixes for the generator, highest-leverage first (block/gate items first). The generator optimizes against these; the grade is re-derived independently each round, so addressing every finding does not mechanically buy a pass.

finding.description: What is wrong, missing, or excess. Be specific; cite the number or figure element.

proposed_action.description: Concrete next step. Make it executable.

target_artifact.description: The sandbox file the action would touch, e.g. iter2/plot.py.

evidence.description: data row, figure element, quoted number, or lit snippet that grounds the finding.

rationale.description: Brief chain-of-thought justifying the grade. Filled AFTER the scores (not score-first-rationalize-after).

score.description: Anchored 1-5 (see rubrics/rubric.md). 5 = publication-quality on this axis; 1 = severely deficient.

justification.description: Why this score, grounded in specifics — the critic's own assessment.

evidence.description: figure element, data value, journal-spec line, or lit snippet the score rests on.

blocking_issues.description: Any confirmed block-level defects on this axis (also surfaced in gate_failures).
